title: Braintree man sentenced to life in prison for murdering Salem State student 40 years ago
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, was sentenced to life without parole for the 1986 murder of Claire Gravel in Essex County.
Carey strangled Gravel to death after she was dropped off at her apartment; her body was found on Route 128.
Carey was convicted based on DNA evidence found on Gravel's clothing; he was already in prison for another crime.

title: ‘It’s pure excitement’: What James Hagens said after first practice with Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, 19, could debut for the Bruins against the Lightning on Saturday after signing an entry-level contract.
Hagens focuses on acclimating to the Bruins, emphasizing learning team dynamics before considering his immediate impact.
Coach Marco Sturm is cautious about Hagens's NHL debut, prioritizing the player's readiness and team needs.

title: Report: 76ers star Joel Embiid to undergo surgery for appendicitis
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joel Embiid will undergo surgery for appendicitis, as reported on Thursday.
The 76ers are currently in seventh place with five games remaining in the NBA season.
Embiid averaged 26.9 points, 7.7 rebounds, and 3.9 assists in 38 games this season.

title: State commits $4M to address substance use crisis at Mass. and Cass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts commits $4 million to combat substance use at Boston's Mass. and Cass area, supporting housing and treatment programs.
Funding allocation includes $2.24 million for housing, $650,000 for clinical support, and $500,000 for a pre-arraignment diversion program.
Over 600 people accessed inpatient treatment in the past year; opioid-related deaths in Boston decreased 38% to lowest since 2015.

title: Family of Stephenson King Jr., the man killed by Boston police officer now charged with manslaughter, demands transparency
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Stephenson King Jr. was fatally shot by Boston Police Officer Nicholas O'Malley on March 11, 2026.
King's family, alongside attorney Ben Crump, demands release of officer body-camera footage for transparency.
Officer Nicholas O'Malley, charged with manslaughter, has pleaded not guilty and is supported by a GoFundMe page.

title: Her first marathon is for her dad and grandfather — lifelong South Boston residents
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Olivia Dennett, 22, is running her first Boston Marathon representing South Boston Neighborhood House, also known as The Ollie.
Dennett's family has deep ties to South Boston; her grandfather opened Joseph’s Bakery there in 1967.
Running honors her late father and grandfather, both lifelong South Boston residents and community pillars.

title: She’s running Boston for her mom and cancer research
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Sophie Goad is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Goad's fundraising goal is $15,124, contributing to the Dana-Farber Marathon Challenge team's $8.75 million target.
Her mother survived clear cell odontogenic carcinoma, inspiring Goad's commitment to cancer research funding.

title: Jaylen Brown says underwater training with a surfing legend helped him deal with anxiety
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown credits underwater training with Laird Hamilton for helping him manage pressure and anxiety.
Brown's regimen included drills simulating being pulled underwater, emphasizing control under stress.
The Celtics star's improved performance includes averaging 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists this season.

title: Police search for owner, dog after man and dog are attacked in Everett
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Police are searching for a dog owner after a dog attack on Ferry St. in Everett injured a man and his dog.
The injured dog, named Mushroom, required multiple surgeries and is recovering at BluePearl veterinary hospital in Charlestown.
The owner of the attacking dog fled before first responders arrived; both human and canine victims are expected to recover.

title: Mass. House passes bill restricting teen social media and phones in schools
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts House passes bill with 129-25 vote banning phone use in public schools during school hours.
Bill requires parental consent for 14-15 year-olds to access social media; bars under 14 from using social media.
Social media age verification systems and updated district phone policies needed by Sept. 1 annually.
